title: cathartolinum alternum
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", cathartolinum alternum, or Alternanthera sessilis, benefits from its ability to exert a spasmolytic effect, relax spastic contractions, and act as a calcium channel blocker, which can help treat conditions such as diarrhea, asthma, and hypertension.
side_effects: Cathartolinum alternum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe abdominal cramps, diarrhea, and dehydration due to its high purgative properties. Prolonged use can lead to nutrient deficiencies, electrolyte imbalances, and potentially life-threatening conditions like hypokalemia. In rare cases, individuals may experience allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Improper use can also cause gastrointestinal perforation, which may require emergency medical attention.
